prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "C-Skip 跑姿练习" (C-skip running drill) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the SIGNATURE 'CYCLING DOWN' phase of a C-skip drill: right foot on the ball of the foot supporting on the ground. The LEFT LEG is the active drill leg and is in mid-cycle: the left thigh is descending from horizontal back down toward the standing position, AND the left lower leg (shin) is bent and curling backward like the bottom-back portion of a bicycle pedal stroke — making a 'C' shape arc. So the left thigh is angled about 30-45 degrees from vertical (going down) AND the left knee is bent so that the left foot is curled back and below the left knee. This is the back-half of a cycling motion. Arms in running-style opposition. Torso upright with slight forward lean. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the leg is in mid-DESCENDING-CYCLE — thigh going DOWN from horizontal and shin curled BACK — NOT thigh held high (that would be A-skip), NOT shin extended forward (that would be B-skip).. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors and quadriceps and hamstring of the cycling leg, plus calf muscles of the supporting leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the hip flexors and quadriceps; "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstring group; "小腿 / 臀大肌" pointing to the calf and glute area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
